title: Flower Lanterns
desc: The glowing indigo blooms around the grove's edge are being collected for evening lamps, and the hanging cups need a cleaner hand than usual.

steps.text:
Along the grove's outer edge, where the sand begins winning again, a line of glowing indigo flowers opens late in the day and is harvested for the little evening lantern cups hung between the trees. The flowers are not bright enough to replace fire. They simply cast a cool low light that makes the grove seem willing to continue existing after sunset. Tonight's collection baskets are ready, but half the lantern cups still hang dusty from the last cycle and the bloom line has started nodding open.

The bloom keeper holds up a pair of thin gloves already stained at the fingertips. "Pick the fresh flowers if you want the beds," she says. "Or clean and hang the lantern cups if you prefer the line. Either way, dusk is coming faster than the blossoms are."

options.label: Pick the fresh glowing flowers along the grove edge
Clean and hang the lantern cups between the trees

steps.text:
The flowers are delicate at the stem and stubborn at the root, which means each one must be pinched free with more care than speed. You move the bed row carefully, collecting the blooms into padded baskets while their cool indigo light gathers under your hands. The outer grove darkens by degrees as you work, the opened blossoms bright enough to mark the edges of each root mound before you lift them.

When the baskets are full, the keeper peels back the cloth and the whole collection glows softly in a pooled blue-violet mass. It is a beautiful sight and an evidently useful one. "Good," she says. "People think these flowers are for mood. Really they are for finding your own feet after dark."

options.label: Carry the bloom baskets to the hanging line

steps.text:
The lantern cups are little glazed holders tied in rows between the grove trees so the evening light can hang above eye level and leave the roots below in readable shadow. Dust, old pollen, and dried sap have filmed the cups dull. You wipe them clear, retie the hanging line where one section has sagged, and space the cups evenly so the evening glow will travel through the grove in a proper sequence instead of a set of guesswork gaps.

By the time you finish, the keeper has begun setting the fresh blooms into the cleaned cups. One by one the line wakes. Soft indigo lights appear between the trees, enough to edge every trunk and root in low calm color. "Better," she says. "A place like this should never become ordinary just because the sun has stopped helping."

options.label: Step back from the line and leave the grove lit for dusk
